title: Kobalos Backdoor Investigation Exercise
description: Investigate a Kobalos backdoor infection in our sandbox environment.

levels.title: Introduction to the Kobalos Backdoor Investigation Exercise
levels.content: Welcome to the Kobalos Backdoor Investigation Exercise! Your mission is to investigate a suspected Kobalos backdoor infection in our sandbox environment. The malware has been observed targeting high-performance computing infrastructure and has been linked to various cyber attacks. You will be provided with access to the affected system, logs, and indicators of compromise (IOCs). Your goal is to identify the initial access vector, lateral movement, persistence mechanisms, and command and control (C2) communication.

levels.title: Accessing the Sandbox Environment
levels.cloud_content: Connect to the target system using SSH and start your investigation.
levels.local_content: Use the provided credentials to access the sandbox environment.

levels.title: Initial Access Vector Investigation
levels.content:
Analyze the logs on the Target system to identify the initial access vector used by the Kobalos backdoor. Look for SSH-related log entries indicating a brute force attack or unusual login activity.

> **Critic revision**: flag at `see solution` on `Target`. This flag is correct and accessible on the Target host
levels.solution: Search for SSH log entries with failed attempts, unusual IP addresses, or suspicious usernames.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Check the /var/log/auth.log file for any signs of brute force attacks.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Look for unusual IP addresses or usernames in the SSH log files.

levels.title: Lateral Movement Investigation
levels.content:
Investigate the Target system for signs of lateral movement by the Kobalos backdoor. Look for any unusual file transfers or network connections.

> **Critic revision**: flag at `see solution` on `Target`. This flag is correct and accessible on the Target host
levels.solution: Check for files with suspicious names or modified timestamps, and examine netstat output for active connections.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Look for any unusual file transfers on the Target system.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Examine netstat output for active connections on the Target system.

levels.title: Command and Control (C2) Communication Investigation
levels.content:
Analyze the Target system's network traffic to identify any communication with the Kobalos backdoor’s command and control server.

> **Critic revision**: flag at `see solution` on ``. This flag is correct and accessible through network traffic analysis
levels.solution: Look for outgoing connections on port 7070, which is known to be used by the Kobalos C2 server.

hints.title: Hint 1
hints.content: Check the Target system's network traffic for any outgoing connections on port 7070.

hints.title: Hint 2
hints.content: Use tools like Wireshark or tcpdump to analyze the Target system's network traffic.
